title: Hat-trick, GOTO secures top net foreign again
body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) records its hat-trick after securing first place at net foreign for three consecutive weeks until the end of today’s (11/12) session.

title: Dividend in sight, IPCM indicates a price uptrend
body: PT Jasa Armada Indonesia Tbk (IPCM) climbed IDR 2 higher this afternoon (11/12) following the announcement of interim dividend distribution.

title: Capital adequacy of Bank IBK Indonesia reaches over 40%
body: PT Bank IBK Indonesia Tbk (AGRS) recorded capital adequacy ratio (CAR) of over 40%.

title: BI survey expects inflation increase in January 2025
body: Inflation is projected to grow in January 2025 before declining in April 2025, according to the Retail Sales Survey of Bank Indonesia in November 2024.

title: Morning session caught EXCL and FREN declining
body: Both PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) were seen corrected at the end of morning session today (11/12) following the merger plan.

title: Hat-trick, GOTO secures top net foreign again
body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) records its hat-trick after securing first place at net foreign for three consecutive weeks until the end of today’s (11/12) session.

title: Dividend in sight, IPCM indicates a price uptrend
body: PT Jasa Armada Indonesia Tbk (IPCM) climbed IDR 2 higher this afternoon (11/12) following the announcement of interim dividend distribution.

title: Capital adequacy of Bank IBK Indonesia reaches over 40%
body: PT Bank IBK Indonesia Tbk (AGRS) recorded capital adequacy ratio (CAR) of over 40%.

title: BI survey expects inflation increase in January 2025
body: Inflation is projected to grow in January 2025 before declining in April 2025, according to the Retail Sales Survey of Bank Indonesia in November 2024.

title: Morning session caught EXCL and FREN declining
body: Both PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) were seen corrected at the end of morning session today (11/12) following the merger plan.

title: DBS and Japanese banks reportedly eyeing Bank Panin acquisition
body: Several prominent Asian banks are reportedly submitting proposals to acquire PT Bank Pan Indonesia Tbk (PNIN), also known as Bank Panin.

title: Foreign buy and sell reached 4 billion shares; net foreign volume closed at 112 million shares
body: On Tuesday, December 10, the foreign buy and sell volumes were both in the range of 4 billion shares, with 4.10 billion shares sold and 4.21 billion shares bought. This made the net foreign volume surplus at 112.56 million shares.

title: GOTO maintains top spot in net foreign transactions
body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) has maintained its position at the top of net foreign transactions as of the close of trading on Tuesday (10/12).

title: IBK Indonesia confident of scoring IDR 250 billion of profit this year
body: PT Bank IBK Indonesia Tbk (AGRS) posted a 9.75% increase in net profit for Q3-2024 compared to the same period last year, reaching IDR 170.02 billion.

title: Gearing up towards Indonesia Emas 2045, Ministry of Energy plans nickel reserves boost
body: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources (ESDM) is preparing strategies to boost nickel reserves in anticipation of Indonesia Emas 2045.

title: Prospects for Indonesian coffee industry promising, Fore opened 216 outlets in 43 cities
body: The prospects for the coffee industry in Indonesia are considered quite promising, encouraging Fore Coffee to open 216 outlets spread across 43 cities in Indonesia.

title: Anthracite diversification as key: DOID's outlook post-ACG acquisition
body: PT Delta Dunia Makmur Tbk (DOID), a mining company and mining services contractor, is optimistic about 2025 after successfully acquiring several mines outside Indonesia.

title: End of first session, DPUM ranks first in top gainers
body: The share performance of PT Dua Putra Utama Makmur Tbk (DPUM) rose 33% from the opening bell until the close of share transactions on Tuesday afternoon (10/12)

title: Expanding to culinary business, Djarum Group to acquire Bakmi GM
body: The Djarum Group will acquire 85% of the shares of PT Griya MieSejati, the parent company of the oldest noodle restaurant chain in Indonesia known as Bakmi GM.

title: Airlangga Hartarto unveils government strategy to secure 8% economic growth
body: Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs, Airlangga Hartarto, revealed several strategies that the Indonesian government is preparing to accelerate economic growth to 8% over the next five years.

title: Manoj Dhamoo Punjabi’s stake in FILM drops by 4.9%
body: Manoj Dhamoo Punjabi's ownership in PT MD Entertainment Tbk (FILM) decreased by 4.9% following a share sale transaction last week (3/12).

title: INDY branches out to fuel trading
body: PT Indika Energy Tbk (INDY) is further diversifying its business portfolio by entering the fuel trading sector, aiming to support future revenue growth.

title: Domestic investors dominated 92% of transaction value
body: Domestic investors accounted for 92% of the total transaction value on the Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) on Monday (9/12).

title: Lippo sets up jumbo rights issue for Meikarta following IDR 1.6 trillion loss
body: PT Lippo Cikarang Tbk (LPCK) is undertaking a significant capital increase through a rights issue to continue financing its Meikarta project, following a loss of IDR1.6 trillion.

title: JCI only rose 0.74% earlier this week, Barito Group occupied top laggards
body: The Composite Stock Price Index (IHSG) on Monday (9/12) rose 0.74% or 54.9 points to 7,437.73, supported by the performance of shares from almost all sectors.

title: Matahari to close 13 outlets, foreign investors posts net sell of 2.08 million shares
body: PT Matahari Department Store Tbk (LPPF)'s plan to close 13 Matahari outlets this year and closely monitor 20 outlets on the watchlist, was responded by foreign investors selling.

title: TMPO rose 30% this Monday
body: PT Tempo Inti Media Tbk (TMPO) saw 30% jump in stock price, securing top gainers position until the closing bell today (9/12).

title: Opening the week, GOTO tops net foreign again this Monday
body: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) recorded net foreign of 2.14 billion shares following its steady uptrend until today’s (9/12) closing.

title: Foreign buy volume surpasses 8 billion shares, net foreign volume surplus at 1.58 billion shares
body: On Monday, December 9, foreign buy volume was approximately 8.28 billion shares, while foreign sell volume was 6.70 billion shares. This gap led to a net foreign volume surplus of 1.58 billion shares.

title: In pharmaceutical industry, SIDO recorded highest net profit in third quarter 2024
body: PT Industri Jamu dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) recorded the top net profit margin among its 13 competitors during January-September 2024.

title: Eyeing new income source, WOOD to work with CTRA on Sidoarjo landbank
body: PT Integra Indocabinet Tbk (WOOD) partners with PT Ciputra Development Tbk (CTRA) to work on green housing project on its landbank located in Lingkar Timur Wedoro Klurak, Sidoarjo, East Java.

title: PTRO to offer bond and sukuk, proceeds allocation revealed
body: PT Petrosea Tbk (PTRO) will allocate IDR 600 billion of the proceeds of Bond and Sukuk Ijarah offering worth IDR 1.5 trillion for material purchase.

title: Planning a stock split, SAMF saw IDR 20 climb
body: PT Saraswati Anugerah Makmur Tbk (SAMF) is confirming the stock split with 1:2 ratio to take place on February 5, 2025.

title: Allowing more pre-opening stocks, IHSG saw 0.36% increase this morning
body: Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) had modified the regulation regarding stocks tradable during pre-opening and pre-closing periods.

title: Gaining strides since morning, JARR seizes top gainer position
body: The price of PT Jhonlin Agro Raya Tbk (JARR) managed to score 24% increase from the opening bell to the end of morning session today (9/12) at Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX).

title: Down 6.12% in a week, BUMI lowers production target
body: PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) announced the revised coal production target to 76-78 million ton throughout 2024 from the previous 78-82 million ton.

title: Retiring its supermarket business, HERO is now DFI Retail Nusantara
body: PT Hero Supermarket Tbk (HERO) has officially changed its name to PT DFI Retail Nusantara Tbk following a successful meeting of shareholders last week.

title: Daily transaction frequency rose, IHSG saw 3.77% jump in a week
body: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (IHSG) posted 3.77% or 268.51-point increase to 7,382.78 last week following growth in daily transaction frequency.

title: BIPI secures place at top of net foreign
body: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) posted net foreign 100.14 million, the highest among other issuers' net foreign at the close of share transactions on Friday (6/12)

title: TMPO reigns Top Gainers by week's closing
body: PT Tempo Inti Media Tbk (TMPO) recorded the highest increase among listed stocks, surging 34.43% by the close of trading on Friday (6/12).
